programmes.show_description: Terry is not very keen on having to mind a racehorse during a trip to the seaside - that is, until he meets a jockey he takes a shine to. S2 E1: National Pelmet
Life in the Reads' household is very different for Alf - especially when, as he gets back from work, Min is in no rush to make his dinner. S7 E2: Min the Housekeeper
The flatmates plan a party to take advantage of the fact that the Ropers will be out visiting Mildred's sister and her husband; the evening begins well but turns sour. S2 E1: While the Cat's Away
Several months have passed - Amy is working, but David is still unemployed and lies to her that his doctor has told him he will have a nervous breakdown unless he takes a holiday. S3 E1: Winter Break
Ozzy tries to build a fireplace near his beach house, but it doesn't go too well. S2 E3: The Ozz Man and the Sea
The workers at the pickle factory go on strike when told they cannot use the new executive washroom. S3 E4: The Power Behind The Throne
Eli is planning a weekend away while Nellie wants to decorate the cellar, but an error means that Eli and Nellie are stuck together for the weekend. S3 E5: Getting To Know You
Railway history and George Stephenson are intertwined. The legendary engineer almost single-handedly created the country we know today.

A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Stan's reputation as an ace mechanic is soon put to the test when Arthur acquires a second-hand motorcycle combination. S2 E2: The Used Combination
The workers of Hatley railway station live a gentle and predictable life, but that is rudely interrupted by some alarming news and a new station master. S1 E1: All Change
Harry the signalman has fattened up a lovely turkey in time for Christmas, but the turkey seems to have other plans. S1 E2: Talking Turkey
New station master Cecil Parkin is intent on making an impression at Hatley, but Jack is sure that he's already made an impression on his wife May. S1 E3: The Train Now Standing
Harry Lambert has been on duty all night in the signal box when the delivery of a body causes problems - should it be classed as freight or passenger? S1 E5: The Late Mr Buckly
Sharon's living back in her council flat and Tracey's still in Chigwell with younger son Travis, but what's become of Dorien and what can bring the Birds back together? S10 E1: Gimme Shelter
Sharon finds that looking after Dorien's every wish is a full-time job - with money tight, Sharon and Tracey take Dorien's designer clothes to a car boot sale to raise funds. S10 E2: Slave
Garth and Marcie aren't getting any 'together time' so it's all change in the bedrooms again - Sharon tries to bed down in the loft, but she makes an unexpected discovery. S10 E3: Hot Stuff
Tracey, Sharon and Dorien each get romantically involved with three very different men, and Travis has romantic troubles of his own. S10 E4: Hearts For Sale
Tracey becomes self-conscious about her appearance and has Botox, Travis gets a tattoo to impress a girl at school, and Sharon looks as if she'll need a hearing aid. S10 E5: Tattoo You
Sharon gets a visit from estranged husband Chris who is seeking a quick divorce so that he can marry his new love Angie - a blind woman - but Tracey has a few doubts. S10 E6: Blinded By Love
Sharon, Tracey and Dorien all lose their jobs and the resulting financial crisis leads to a huge row - some home truths are revealed and the three of them hit rock bottom. S10 E7: Back To Zero
Sharon and Tracey get a glimpse of a possible future when they see two elderly sisters in the supermarket, and Dorien's court case comes to a conclusion. S10 E8: You Can't Always Get What You Want
Dorien gets the idea that Sharon could be the heroine for her next book, and Tracey's got her hands full with a despondent Garth. S11 E2: There's Something About Sharon
Sharon and Tracey discover that Dorien has been keeping a secret for over fifty years and decide to get involved, but Dorien is reluctant to confront her past. S11 E3: Guess Who's Coming to Essex?
Tracey gets a letter from her auntie Vera who is retiring as a Pearly Queen and wants her to take over, but Tracey faces competition from a jealous Sharon. S11 E4: The Girls With The Pearl Buttons
George's car fails its MOT and he needs £20 to get it fixed - he sells his piano to Robin, but the problem is how to get it upstairs. S5 E2: Right Said George
George needs help finding the answers for all the competitions he enters - luckily, Robin is starting a new job selling encyclopaedias and is looking for his first buyer. S5 E3: A Little Knowledge
While the flatmates compete to get the flat to themselves to entertain friends, George is trying to find jobs to avoid Mildred. S5 E4: Love and Let Love

When Mildred enters a flower arranging competition, Chrissy offers her a plant from her garden; when Larry sees one in Chrissy's flat, he thinks it's a cannabis plant. S5 E5: How Does Your Garden Grow
Chrissy receives two tickets for a Frank Sinatra concert but does not want them; she instead enjoys being pampered by Jo and Robin as they compete for the tickets. S5 E6: Come Fly With Me
The youngsters want a party, but George - recalling the last one - is against the idea. Mildred, however, is all for it and gives it her blessing... S6 E1: The Party's Over
When the lease is up on the flat and George wants to increase the rent, the flatmates decide that the only way to raise the money is to get a fourth tenant to share. S6 E2: One More for the Pot
Arthur receives a couple of interesting propositions while attending Charlie Johnson's funeral. S8 E5: Guess Who's Coming to Pinner
A lawyer from America has unbelievable news for Madge and the Garveys, with life-changing consequences. This lawyer arouses the attention of Solana staff, and Joyce has Leslie investigate. S7 E2
After Mandy gets arrested for suspected drug running, she forms the most unlikely of friendships and finds herself helping Tom search for his real mother. S5 E14
Chrissy's new boyfriend wants to take her to Bournemouth for the weekend, but Robin discovers something about him that he thinks might make Chrissy want to reconsider. S2 E3: In Praise of Older Men
Could rumours of the Grey Lady who haunts the house be true? Rigsby certainly believes so, but he has not seen her yet... S2 E7: Things That Go Bump In The Night
A lawyer from America has unbelievable news for Madge and the Garveys, with life-changing consequences. This lawyer arouses the attention of Solana staff, and Joyce has Leslie investigate. S7 E2
Arthur's friend Alex has been the victim of a hit-and-run incident - Terry agrees to 'mind' his antique shop, but is his business partner up to no good? S2 E2: Whose Wife Is It Anyway?
Min orders Bert to lose weight by jogging. Alf keeps pace on a bike, but before long they end up in the pub. S7 E3: Drunk in Charge of a Bicycle
When Chrissy is harassed by an intimidating tall man at the pub, Robin decides that he will learn self-defence and take the man on the next time he sees him. S2 E2: Colour Me Yellow
David is caught out when Amy tells him that Tommy Wright is dead, so he and Linda adopt disguises to avoid each other's spouses as they plan to move to another hotel. S3 E2: Deja Vu
Kelly meets a boy called Bert but refuses to introduce him to Sharon for fear of embarrassment. S2 E4: Beauty and the Bert
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
With growing competition, the factory faces closure, leaving Eli and Nellie penniless, starving, and with no staff. S3 E6: Two Pennies To Rub Together
It is Boxing Day in the Pledge house, Bored Lily and Walter join Nellie and Eli in a seance. S3 E7: The Ghost of Picklers Past
Throughout the Heartlands there are many small railways which serve the market towns and villages and bring prosperity to their people.

A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Stan and the busmen of the Luxton and District Traction Company take karate lessons; fortunately for them, their Inspector is an expert in the art... S2 E3: Self Defence
A horsebox belonging to Lady Lawrence is delivered to the station - and Wilfred is on hand with the essentials - while Harry's signal box seems to be on fire. S1 E6: Horse Play
The staff are up early to greet a new arrival at Hatley Station - another arrival later in the day is less welcome... S1 E7: Past Love
May is keen to make a career move to a bigger station, but both Jack and Cecil are set against her going and both try to apply for a transfer to be near her. S1 E8: Job Opportunity
Jack and May have problems with their teenage daughter, and it's dance night and Ethel is excited about hoping to find love at last. S1 E9: Sleeping Around
Gary Sparrow is an unhappily married TV repairman who one day walks into a time warp. Nicholas Lyndhurst stars in the hit 1990s sitcom. S1 E1: Rites of Passage
Gary returns to the bar to begin courting Phoebe, where he runs into her husband Donald. S1 E2: Fools Rush In
Gary arouses suspicions when he saves Phoebe from an early evening bombing raid. S1 E3: Is Your Journey Really Necessary?
Ron agrees to help Gary return to 1940 but insists he go with him, and Phoebe loses half her belongings in a bombing. S1 E4: The More I See You
Gary believes that he married Phoebe after learning of an elderly woman name Phoebe Sparrow. S1 E5: I Get Along Without You Very Well
Yvonne delivers an ultimatum to Gary, but Ron makes him an offer he can't refuse. Will he return to 1942, and if he does, will he ever come back to the 1990s?S1 E6: In the Mood
When Gary needs money for a new house, Ron convinces him to try his investment idea for the second time. S2 E1: Don't Get Around Much Any More
Gary is left feeling unwanted when a Czech officer takes an interest in Phoebe. S2 E2: I Got It Bad and That Ain't Good
Gary is left feeling unwanted when a Czech officer takes an interest in Phoebe. S2 E3: Just One More Chance
Gary attempts to have simultaneous dinner dates with Yvonne and Phoebe with a little help from Ron. S2 E4: Who's Taking You Home Tonight?
When Mildred is going to the Floral Society annual dinner dance but George doesn't want to, Robin agrees to accompany Mildred to the dance and leaves George rather jealous. S6 E3: The Generation Game
George needs some money and so sells Mildred's sun lamp to Larry; later, when Mildred is upstairs, she sees her lamp and thinks that Robin has stolen it. S6 E4: The Sunshine Boys
Robin's brother Norman comes to stay for two days and takes a shine to Chrissy; when the three of them go out to dinner, Robin gets food poisoning. S6 E5: Mum Always Liked You Best
Chrissy and Norman have been dating for some weeks to the annoyance of Robin, who goes on a disastrous double date with Larry. S6 E6: Fire Down Below

Mildred is upset that she has not received an invitation to Norman and Chrissy's wedding, but George has hidden it from her because he wants to go to a darts match. S6 E7: Another Bride, Another Groom
When Arthur's GP orders him to cut down on booze and cigars, his grip on reality is called into question. S8 E6: The Last Temptation of Daley
Joyce becomes suspicious when Blow and Go appears extremely busy. Tiger hides the news that Auntie Terri is about to join them at The Solana. The Oracle and his mum arrive back. S7 E3
Norma wakes up in a stranger's jogging bottoms after a night on the tiles and sets off on a wild goose chase to find out who she slept with the night before. S5 E14
The Ropers are invited for a meal not realising that Robin has invited his German friend Franz; the meal does not go well as George has a grudge against Germans. S2 E4: Did You Ever Meet Rommel?
Classic sitcom about a miserly landlord. Rigsby faces the prospect of a lonely Christmas until Alan and Philip arrive unexpectedly. S2 E8: For the Man Who Has Everything (Christmas Special 1975)
Joyce becomes suspicious when Blow and Go appears extremely busy. Tiger hides the news that Auntie Terri is about to join them at The Solana. The Oracle and his mum arrive back. S7 E3
